items.title: [AINews] SpaceXAI launches Grok 4.5, first Opus-class model post Cursor acquisition
items.summary: SpaceXAI continues to move faster than any other frontier lab on earth.

items.title: Why AI Infrastructure must evolve for Agent Experience — Akshat Bubna, Modal CTO
items.summary: 2 years after our first coverage, we return with Modal's other cofounder to explore why Agent Experience is working now, and everything they have learned building the new agent cloud.

items.title: [AINews] Lilian Weng summarizes 35 papers on Harness Engineering for RSI
items.summary: a quiet day lets us read some condensed insight

items.title: [AINews] The Field Guide to Fable
items.summary: a quiet day lets us digest the world's most significant model launch... to date.

items.title: AIEWF Daily Dispatch: The great loops debate and the state of AI engineering
items.summary: The AI Engineer World’s Fair ended with a debate about loops, a report on the state of AI engineering, and closing keynotes focused on what to build next.

items.title: Vercel's Andrew Qu on why agents are a new kind of software
items.summary: The Vercel Chief of Software explains how its agent framework, eve, was created — and why skills, sandboxes and agent-readable websites now matter.

items.title: The website of the future may assemble itself for every visitor
items.summary: Adobe is experimenting with “agentic sites” that generate pages around an individual user’s intent. At AIEWF, we talked to Carlos Sanchez about the Web's future.

items.title: Skill engineering and the case against one-shot AI design
items.summary: Paul Bakaus talks to us about Impeccable, human judgment in a 'loopmaxxing' era, and why agents still need people to steer them.

items.title: AIEWF Daily Dispatch: Autoresearch and the tension between AI and human agency
items.summary: The software factory vision met resistance today from speakers defending human understanding and control.
